generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleMotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts speed commands into motor voltage/current commands for powertrain actuation in the 1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle"
]

instance SteeringServoActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls steering via servo mechanism for lane-keeping and adaptive navigation in the autonomous vehicle"
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Implements ACC algorithm execution for dynamic speed regulation and obstacle avoidance"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance VehicleSpeedSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures real-time vehicle speed data for state monitoring in the autonomous vehicle"
]

instance VehiclePositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks vehicle position and trajectory data for navigation and localization"
]

instance MotorActuationFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides feedback on motor response data for closed-loop control validation"
]

instance SteeringServoFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Records steering servo signals and response data for precision control validation"
]

instance LiDARPointCloudSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generates LiDAR point clouds for spatial mapping and obstacle detection using Hokuyo UST-10LX"
]

instance DepthCameraSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides depth camera data (Intel Deepsense D435i) for multimodal spatial mapping integration"
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleSpeedSensor, VehiclePositionSensor
]

instance MotorCommandsData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleMotorActuator
]

instance SteeringCommandsData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoActuator
]

instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor
]

instance ActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationFeedbackSensor, SteeringServoFeedbackSensor
]

instance SteeringServoFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoFeedbackSensor
]

// C5: Data processing components
instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Data Translation FE serializes and aligns ROS2 message types (e.g., ROS2 topics) while performing unit conversions for inter-system data exchange"
]

instance PointCloudProcessingFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Performs DBSCAN clustering and tracking on LiDAR point clouds for dynamic OME tracking"
]

instance DataCollectingFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Handles structured data storage and report generation for vehicle telemetry and DT state data"
]

instance ReportingFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Generates user-facing reports and visualizations for vehicle operation monitoring"
]

// C6: Models/Data
instance GeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric representation of the 1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle chassis and powertrain subsystem using CAD or point cloud structures"
]

instance BehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Functional entity-based behavioral model implementing control logic for vehicle dynamics and perception"
]

instance AdaptiveControlCoefficientModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Real-time ACC algorithm model for dynamic speed regulation and obstacle avoidance optimization"
]

instance VehicleStateDataStore : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured storage of vehicle state data (speed, position, acceleration) for historical analysis and monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
]

instance MotorActuationDataStore : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data storage for motor actuation commands and feedback for closed-loop control validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCommandsData, ActuationFeedbackData
]

instance LiDARProcessingDataStore : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed LiDAR point cloud data after DBSCAN clustering and tracking for spatial mapping"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance ActuationFeedbackDataStore : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Feedback data from motor and steering servo systems for system performance validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationFeedbackData
]

// C7: Data flows to enablers
VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE, DataCollectingFE
MotorCommandsData : DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
SteeringCommandsData : DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingFE, DataCollectingFE
ActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE, DataCollectingFE

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2MiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based middleware framework enabling modular communication between sensors, AI modules, and DT components with real-time synchronization"
]

instance NVIDIAJetpackEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetpack 5.0 SDK for optimizing AI workloads (ACC algorithm execution) on Jetson edge devices for low-latency processing"
]

instance SQLDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database system for structured storage of vehicle telemetry and DT state data"
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink platform for AI model prototyping and digital twin simulation validation"
]

// C11: Enabler-Services relationships
ROS2MiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
NVIDIAJetpackEnabler : DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
SQLDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:enables DataStorageService
MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:enables SimulationValidationService

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time streaming and aggregation of sensor data (speed, acceleration, environmental obstacles) via ROS2 for state visualization and anomaly detection"
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads heavy computational tasks (ACC algorithm execution) to edge devices (NVIDIA Jetson) for low-latency processing"
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote activation/deactivation of ACC features and dynamic speed setting via cloud-based interfaces for off-site configuration"
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides predictive maintenance alerts for sensor degradation detection based on telemetry analysis"
]

instance SimulationOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables simulation-driven optimization of autonomous driving parameters for testing validation"
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state parameters (speed, position, acceleration) for operational monitoring"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detects operational anomalies in sensor data streams for predictive maintenance triggers"
]

instance SpeedRegulationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of vehicle speed based on ACC algorithm execution for dynamic traffic conditions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ObstacleAvoidanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Executive steering and motor commands to avoid detected obstacles in real-time"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SensorDegradationAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "User notification of sensor degradation via predictive maintenance system"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ParameterOptimizationRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Simulation-based recommendations for optimizing autonomous driving parameters"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C12: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains VehicleMotorActuator, SteeringServoActuator, AdaptiveCruiseControlActuator,
                 VehicleSpeedSensor, VehiclePositionSensor, MotorActuationFeedbackSensor,
                 SteeringServoFeedbackSensor, LiDARPointCloudSensor, DepthCameraSensor,
                 DataTranslationFE, PointCloudProcessingFE, DataCollectingFE, ReportingFE,
                 ROS2MiddlewareEnabler, NVIDIAJetpackEnabler, SQLDatabaseEnabler,
                 MATLABSimulinkEnabler, VehicleStateDataStore, MotorActuationDataStore,
                 LiDARProcessingDataStore, ActuationFeedbackDataStore
]

// C13: Physical Twin Entity
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "Hosts resource-specific automotive functionalities including Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking components deployed on hardware managing sensors/actuators"
    base:contains SteeringServoActuator, AdaptiveCruiseControlActuator,
                 LiDARPointCloudSensor, DepthCameraSensor
]

// C14: Digital Twin Entity
instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "Dedicated to data management and control mechanisms with ROS2-based middleware for real-time synchronization between physical and digital domains"
    base:contains DataTranslationFE, PointCloudProcessingFE, DataCollectingFE,
                 ROS2MiddlewareEnabler, NVIDIAJetpackEnabler, SQLDatabaseEnabler,
                 VehicleStateDataStore, MotorActuationDataStore, LiDARProcessingDataStore,
                 ActuationFeedbackDataStore
]

// C15: User Entity
instance UserVisualizationComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "3D visualization interface for user interaction with the Digital Twin system"
]

instance UserControlPanelComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Control panel interface for remote management and configuration of ACC features"
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "Combined user interface entity containing 3D Visualization and Control Panel components for operational monitoring and control"
    base:contains UserVisualizationComponent, UserControlPanelComponent
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  **Note:** The document does not explicitly describe control flows from digital to physical entities. Key inferred gaps:
  - **Command Types:** Not explicitly detailed (e.g., steering commands, motor adjustments, or system overrides).
  - **Control Message Formats:** Not specified (e.g., ROS2 message structures for actuation commands).
  - **Validation & Authentication:** Not mentioned (e.g., security checks for control signals).
  - **Feedback Systems:** Not described (e.g., how digital twin confirms command execution or reports errors).
  - **Conflict Resolution:** Not addressed (e.g., handling conflicting commands or priority rules).
  - **Command Execution Confirmation:** Not outlined (e.g., acknowledgments or status updates from physical systems).

  **Inferred Context from Table:**
    - The **Actuation FE** and **Controlling FE** (in the third row) may handle control signals, but specifics are missing.
    - **Data Translation FE** ensures consistency for both data and control messages, but no details on bidirectional control flows.
  **Conclusion:** Virtual-to-physical interaction is **not in document** for this Digital Twin System (DTS).
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system enforces **strict time synchronization** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading. The system mandates **clock synchronization accuracy of ≤100 ms** for safety-critical vehicular applications, driven by hardware limitations. This temporal granularity ensures real-time alignment for critical operations, particularly where sub-100 ms latency is required for accurate sensor-actuator coordination and data processing (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking). The synchronization framework aligns with industry standards demanding stricter precision than 100 ms, though hardware constraints cap the achievable accuracy at this threshold.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system’s architecture is **ISO 23247 RA-aligned** but introduces **departures**:
- **Component Mapping**: SW components are mapped to Feature Elements (FEs) via ROS2 packages, each hosting ROS2 nodes. This modularization organizes entities (Physical/Digital/User) by ISO 23247’s entity-based structure.
- **Orchestration**: Coordination between entities occurs via **ROS2’s publish-subscribe model**, enabling inter-FE communication. The **Physical Twin Entity** hosts general automotive functions (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering), while the **Digital Twin Entity** manages data-centric workflows.
- **Resource Management**: Each FE is deployed as an **independent ROS2 package**, ensuring isolated execution but requiring explicit integration for cross-entity data flow. The system **does not specify orchestration patterns** (e.g., microservices, event-driven) beyond ROS2’s native capabilities.
- **System-Wide Coordination**: The **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel** are subcomponents of the User Entity, implying a **top-down control hierarchy** where user interactions trigger downstream FE activations. No mention of peer-to-peer coordination or federated twin orchestration exists.
"
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle stages of the Digital Twin (DT) system, as implied by the provided context, are not explicitly detailed in the documents. However, the **ISO 23247 standard** outlines a foundational framework where lifecycle governance is implicitly governed by the **entity-based reference architecture (RA)**. While the documents do not specify formal lifecycle phases (e.g., design, deployment, maintenance), the **development stages** inferred from the referenced demonstration (e.g., adaptive cruise control DT) include:
   - **Definition of Digital Twin System (DTS) requirements** (e.g., functional and non-functional specifications for adaptive cruise control integration).
   - **Architectural design** (mapping Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs) like sensors/actuators to Digital Twin Entities (DTEs) via entity-based RA).
   - **Implementation of DTS services** (e.g., integrating ROS 2 middleware for communication between software components).
   Transition management is handled through modular **Functional Entities (FEs)**, which allow incremental updates without disrupting the entire system. Governance is implicitly enforced via ISO 23247’s **information exchange and entity responsibility framework**, ensuring alignment with manufacturing standards."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The system’s fidelity and validity are governed by:
- **Model Fidelity Requirements**: Implied by ISO 23247’s entity-based architecture, where **Functional Entities (FEs)** (e.g., **ACC FE**) must align with physical OMEs (e.g., vehicle dynamics).
- **Validation/Verification**: Not explicitly detailed in the documents, though **closed-loop control** mechanisms imply iterative validation.
- **Uncertainty Sources**: Not quantified; robustness against noise handled via **DBSCAN** in point cloud processing.
- **Error Detection/Correction**: Implied by FE-based feedback loops (e.g., real-time adjustments in ROS2).
- **Quality Assurance**: Not specified; adherence to **ISO 23247** principles (e.g., modular FE implementation) ensures consistency but lacks explicit cybersecurity/data integrity protocols.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Technical Network Infrastructure:**
  - **Communication Protocols:**
    - **Internal SW Communication:** ROS2 (ROS 2.0) for intra-entity data/control message exchange within software components.
    - **Inter-Entity Communication:** Wi-Fi network with UDP transport layer for physical-to-digital and digital-to-physical data transfer.

  - **Network Topology & Architecture:**
    - **Localized Deployment:** Components appear to operate in a distributed but cohesive architecture (e.g., Fig. 2 shows sensor data flowing through FE modules).
    - **Layered Design:** Separation of data processing (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering), visualization (e.g., 3D Visualization), and control (e.g., Controlling FE).
    - **ROS2 Network:** Likely a centralized or clustered ROS2 network for intra-FE communication.
    - **Wi-Fi Network:** Acts as a bridge between physical sensors (e.g., LiDAR) and digital twin components (e.g., Data Collection FE).

  - **Bandwidth & Latency Requirements:**
    - **Real-time Criticality:** Point cloud processing and vehicle state updates require low-latency communication (e.g., <100ms for LiDAR data).
    - **Bandwidth:** High-throughput for LiDAR point clouds (e.g., 100+ points/sec) and frequent updates (e.g., 10Hz+ for vehicle state).
    - **ROS2 Network:** Designed for low-latency, high-throughput messaging (e.g., ROS2’s publish-subscribe model).
    - **Wi-Fi UDP:** Prioritized for critical data streams (e.g., vehicle state, steering commands).

  - **Reliability & Redundancy Mechanisms:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Built-in redundancy and fault tolerance (e.g., node restarts, message retries).
    - **Wi-Fi Network:** UDP may lack redundancy; potential for packet loss handled by FE components (e.g., Data Translation FE).
    - **Sensor Redundancy:** Not explicitly mentioned, but clustering/tracking FE may mitigate sensor noise.

  - **Security Protocols:**
    - **Data Translation FE:** Ensures unit alignment and message serialization, but **no explicit security protocols** (e.g., encryption, authentication) are described.
    - **ROS2 Network:** May use ROS2’s built-in security (e.g., TLS for message encryption), but not detailed in the document.
    - **Wi-Fi Network:** UDP lacks built-in security; potential for MITM attacks if not mitigated elsewhere (e.g., by physical security).
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  **Deployment Infrastructure:**
  - **Hosting Platforms:**
    - **Edge Computing:** Likely deployed on-device (e.g., within the vehicle) for real-time processing (e.g., LiDAR clustering, actuation FE).
    - **Cloud/On-Premise:** Data storage (e.g., reports, historical data) may reside in centralized systems, but specifics are not provided.
    - **Hybrid Model:** ROS2 network suggests distributed deployment across multiple nodes (e.g., onboard and cloud-based components).

  - **Computational & Storage Requirements:**
    - **Real-time Processing:**
      - **CPU/GPU:** High-performance hardware for point cloud processing (e.g., clustering, tracking) and actuation FE.
      - **Memory:** Large buffers for LiDAR point clouds (e.g., 100K+ points/sec) and intermediate data (e.g., vehicle state).
    - **Storage:**
      - **Raw Data:** Temporary storage for sensor logs (e.g., LiDAR, motor/steering data).
      - **Processed Data:** Persistent storage for reports, visualizations, and historical analysis (e.g., data storage FE).
      - **Bandwidth:** High storage throughput for real-time data collection (e.g., 100MB/s+ for LiDAR streams).

  - **Scalability Mechanisms:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Supports horizontal scaling (e.g., adding more nodes for parallel processing).
    - **Modular FE Components:** Each FE (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering, Reporting FE) can scale independently.
    - **Cloud/Edge Hybrid:** Edge deployment for real-time tasks; cloud for offloading heavy computations (e.g., report generation).

  - **Deployment Models Supported:**
    - **On-Device Deployment:** Critical for real-time vehicle monitoring (e.g., onboard LiDAR processing).
    - **Cloud-Based Deployment:** For non-real-time tasks (e.g., report generation, historical analysis).
    - **Edge-Cloud Hybrid:** Combines real-time edge processing with cloud storage/analytics.

  - **Availability & Reliability Assurance:**
    - **Redundancy:** ROS2 nodes may auto-recover from failures (e.g., node restarts).
    - **Fault Tolerance:** Data Translation FE ensures message consistency even if some nodes fail.
    - **No Explicit SLAs:** Not detailed in the document, but real-time requirements imply high availability (e.g., 99.9% uptime for vehicle monitoring).
    - **Physical Security:** LiDAR and sensors are likely protected (e.g., vehicle-mounted), but digital security (e.g., Wi-Fi encryption) is not specified.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "
Not in Document
"

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines an **entity-based framework** with four core entities: **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**. Each entity is composed of **Functional Entities (FEs)**, though the standard does not mandate specific technologies. The case study (GitHub repository) references **ROS 2** as a framework for implementing FEs, alongside **Wi-Fi** for connectivity. **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** is mentioned as an *example* implementation for FEs, not as a mandatory standard. No certification requirements, compliance frameworks, or interoperability standards beyond ISO 23247 are explicitly documented."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for **Vehicle-to-X (V2X) services** adheres to **ISO 15622** for adaptive cruise control (ACC) implementation, requiring the system to incorporate the specified control strategy. While no explicit mention of cybersecurity frameworks like ISO 26262 or cryptographic protocols (e.g., OAuth 2.0, ECDSA, RSA) is present, the **3GPP TS 22.185** specifies a **network latency constraint of ≤100 ms** for V2X communication between the vehicle and the Digital Twin entity, ensuring real-time safety-critical data exchange. The system’s safety considerations are implicitly tied to functional safety standards (e.g., ISO 26262-1), though no detailed threat mitigation (e.g., intrusion detection systems) or fail-safe mechanisms are explicitly detailed in the provided documents."
]
